title: Xanadu reveals government funding talks as company woos SPAC investors ahead of merger vote - The Globe and Mail
why:
- Xanadu has entered negotiations with the Ontario and federal governments to secure up to $390 million in support for advanced semiconductor and photonic manufacturing initiatives in Canada.
- If successful, Xanadu will become the first Canadian technology company to go public on the Toronto Stock Exchange since 2021 through a SPAC merger, with a potential US$455 million in net cash.
- The deal has attracted US$275 million in private investment, with 90% coming from new backers including major firms such as AMD, BMO Global Asset Management, and CIBC Asset Management.
- Xanadu’s quantum computers use laser light and operate largely at room temperature, differing from other systems requiring extreme cooling.
- Xanadu was the third group to achieve "quantum advantage," performing a calculation in two minutes that would have taken a classical computer seven million years.
- The company has developed open-source quantum software, Penny Lane, with 35,000 active users across various quantum systems.
